title: Megalight
content:
{{Top|canon=Megalight per hour|notoc=1}}
A '''megalight''', abbreviated '''MGLT''', was a standard unit of distance in space.

The [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]] and [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]] had top sublight speeds of 100 MGLT per hour.<ref name="XWA1">''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' and associated manual</ref>

The [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] targeting system of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star I]] used megalights to indicate the distance to [[Planet/Legends|planets]] to be fired upon.<ref name="XWCS1">[[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']] Tour of Duty 3 cutscene 2</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Ilmmglt.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An ILM spaceship speed and maneuverability chart from ''Return of the Jedi'']]
The MGLT was originally introduced in [[Industrial Light & Magic]]'s reference chart for [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] as a measurement of relative speed.<ref name="ROTJS">''[[Return of the Jedi Sketchbook]]''</ref>

In the ''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' interactive CD-ROM, released in the Fall of [[1998]], a "vehicle speed comparison chart" shows the maximum sublight speeds of various spacecraft displayed in MGLT. However, the ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'' instruction manual, from [[1999]], suggests, "To dodge missiles, try flying 90 megalights per hour or faster," which would indicate that MGLT '''per hour''' is the unit of speed, making MGLT itself a unit of distance.
